holes.content: **AHE** -- the framework named in the title -- is proposed as the concrete realisation of that discipline: it specifies the design vocabulary for harnesses (the components and their interfaces), the runtime properties a harness must expose (notably observability), and the lifecycle by which a harness improves itself in response to its own runtime evidence (self-evolution).
holes.label: ahe_realises_harness_engineering

holes.content: The productive unit of engineering for LLM-based agents is the **harness** -- the scaffold of prompts, tools, memory, control loops, and observability that surrounds the base model -- rather than the model weights themselves. As a corollary, the construction and operation of harnesses deserves a dedicated engineering discipline with its own design vocabulary, runtime requirements, and improvement loop, on par with software engineering or site-reliability engineering.
holes.label: harness_is_the_engineering_object
